messages.content:
About 75% of population in Pakistan resides in rural areas and the remaining in peri-urban and urban areas, while the allocated funds for improvement of health care programmes are more for urban areas. Which item of these better explains this example?
(1 Point)


Inequity

Prioritization

Resource generation

Sustainability

Inequality
Inequity

For providing quality health services, community participation plays an essential role to ensure maximum community participation, which level of health care system should be strengthened?
(1 Point)


Primary health care

Secondary health care

Tertiary health care

1st level referral facility

Higher level referral facility
Primary health care

People of a village reported a high prevalence of bacterial gastroenteritis even after proper chlorination of water supply for the recommended duration. On water analysis, level of chlorine in water was 0.01 mg/L and pH of water was 6.5. There were no suspended impurities; levels of sulphides and ferrous were low. What is the likely reason of increased bacterial gastroenteritis even after chlorination?
(1 Point)


Low residual chlorine

High pH leading to chlorination failure

Presence of sulphides

Low level of ferrous

Inadequate contact time
Inadequate contact time

An expert in the field of public health is required to estimate the magnitude of a health problem. 
Which rate would he calculate for this?
(1 Point)


Cause specific mortality

Incidence

Prevalence

Case fatality

Proportionate mortality
Prevalence

The number of deaths due to diarrhoea, total cases of measles, total number of accidents and the total number of drug addicts were to be reported by a researcher. 
What is the best title that could be given to all of this data?
(1 Point)


Health related data

Mortality data 1

Morbidity data

Case fatality rate

Addiction rate
Health related data

In an outbreak of cholera in a village of 2,000 population, 20 cases have occurred and 5 die. 
What is the Case fatality rate? 


(1 Point)


1%

0.25%

5%

25%

0.0025%

Case fatality rate is calculated by dividing the number of deaths by the number of cases, and then multiplying by 100.

In this case, the number of deaths is 5 and the number of cases is 20.

Case fatality rate = (5/20) * 100 = 25%

Therefore, the correct answer is 25%.

What is the most important reason for recommending oral polio vaccine in the polio eradication campaign despite availability of a safe injectable vaccine?
(1 Point)


Provides 90% immunity in one dose

Does not interfere with vertical immunity

Has been donated by WHO

Provides herd immunity

Has less side effects
Provides herd immunity

What is the most important reason for recommending oral polio vaccine in the polio eradication campaign despite availability of a safe injectable vaccine?
(1 Point)


Provides 90% immunity in one dose

Does not interfere with vertical immunity

Has been donated by WHO

Provides herd immunity

Has less side effects
Provides herd immunity

10 cases of food poisoning had been reported in hospital, 2 out of these developed mild gastrointestinal symptoms, 4 developed moderate dehydration but recovered and 2 succumbed to the disease. 
What is the  characteristic of the organism of food poisoning that produces the severest form of the disease?
(1 Point)


Infectivity

Pathogenicity

Virulence

Communicability

Resistibility
Virulence

A mother brought her six weeks old child to an EPI center for routine immunization. She was enquired about history of Epilepsy in the family and febrile fits. Which type of these immunizations did the doctor took this history to avoid complication with?
(1 Point)


Diphtheria toxoid

Tetanus toxoid

Hepatitis B vaccine

Pertussis vaccine

OPV
Pertussis vaccine

What does Early diagnosis & prompt treatment focus on?
 
(1 Point)


Disease identification

Host factors

Environmental factors

Restoration of ability

Behaviour modification
Disease identification.
What does Early diagnosis & prompt treatment focus on?
Early diagnosis & prompt treatment focuses on disease identification.

Higher crude annual mortality rate in a developing country as compared to a developed country is mostly due to which one of the following reasons?
 
(1 Point)


An incorrect record keeping

A younger age distribution

An inaccurate census of the population.

More stressful life style

Greater exposure to occupational hazards.
A greater exposure to occupational hazards.

Acute hemorrhagic conjunctivitis affected a large proportion of population over a wide geographic area in 1971 and 1981. 
What is the type of spread of this disease?
 
(1 Point)


Epidemic

Sporadic

Pandemic

Endemic

Opportunistic
Epidemic

The Bhopal disaster, also referred to as the Bhopal gas tragedy, was a chemical accident on the night of 2–3 December 1984 at the Union Carbide India Limited (UCIL) pesticide plant in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, India. The industrial disaster is considered the world's worst in history. Over 500,000 people were exposed to methyl isocyanate (MIC) gas. The highly toxic substance made its way into and around the small towns located near the plant. What is the best title that represents this disaster?
(1 Point)


Slow epidemic

Continuous epidemic

Point source epidemic

Propagated epidemic

An accident which did not warrant an emergency
Point source epidemic

In a population of 1000, measles coverage is 60%, one child goes out of station and comes back with measles from whom 20 more children get measles. What is the secondary attack rate of measles? 
(1 Point)


0.65%

5%

6%

6.5%

7%

The secondary attack rate is calculated by dividing the number of new cases (20 in this case) by the number of susceptible individuals (which is the total population minus the one child who went out of station). 

Number of susceptible individuals = 1000 - 1 = 999

Secondary attack rate = (20/999) * 100 = 2.002%

Therefore, the secondary attack rate of measles is approximately 2.002%.
did you take into account that measles coverage is 60 percent?

Apologies for the oversight. You are correct, we need to consider the measles coverage of 60% before calculating the secondary attack rate.

The number of susceptible individuals in the population would be 40% (100% - 60%) of the total population.

Number of susceptible individuals = 0.40 * 1000 = 400

Secondary attack rate = (20/400) * 100 = 5%

Therefore, the corrected secondary attack rate of measles is 5%. Thank you for pointing out the mistake.

A woman in the seventh month of pregnancy reports to you in the antenatal clinic for the first time. 
What is the recommended immunization?
(1 Point)


Tetanus toxoid

Hepatitis B vaccine

Rubella vaccine

Pneumococcal vaccine

Tetanus immunoglobulin
Tetanus toxoid

In a universe comprising of 1500 children less than 5 years of age, 75 children with severe malnutrition were found If 75 new cases of severe malnutrition were registered over a period of one year, 
what is the incidence rate for severe malnutrition during the same year?

The incidence rate is calculated by dividing the number of new cases (75) by the total population at risk (1500).

Incidence rate = (75/1500) * 100 = 5%

Therefore, the incidence rate for severe malnutrition during the year is 5%.

When total number of deaths due to measles is presented in relation to the total cases of measles, 
what is the best label that represents this relation?

The best label that represents the relation between total number of deaths due to measles and the total cases of measles is:

Case fatality rate

A General Practitioner purchased BCG vaccine for his clinic, where should he store this vaccine at his clinic? 
(1 Point)


A dark place

Deep freezer

Water carrier

Refrigerator

Shelf
Refrigerator
